objective: Build the first NeurIPS-grade expanded composition benchmark: a replay-verified non-fixture composition shard with enough generated rows to support meaningful topology/source holdouts, leakage-safe reports, and decomposition-aware baselines tied directly to the decomposition generalization claim.
non_negotiables: The existing Wave 18 default shard remains byte-stable unless an explicit migration is documented; expanded composition data must start behind a separate command or artifact path.
Every exact row in the expanded shard must pass Astralbase replay: board parse, BitMesh conservative legal-independence proof, component CGT values, BMCOMPOSE digest, and result exact-value payload.
Rejected composition controls remain rejected rows and never enter exact target metrics.
Every Partizan report must include source, topology, D4-symmetry, position, decomposition, composition, component-value, component-pair, and result-value leakage checks.

Profile capacity support is not model evidence. Decomposition-benefit claims remain inactive until a decomposition-aware baseline beats full-board, no-decomposition, train-majority, FEN/material, and hand-feature controls on the same expanded holdout.
Agency, aesthetics, and temperature claims remain inactive unless exact nonconstant targets and verified galleries are produced and beat the relevant controls.

tasks.prompt: Add an explicit expanded non-fixture composed-domain shard command that uses the Wave 21 profile pool to emit 10 generated exact rows per depth-two topology family while preserving the existing --non-fixture-composed-domain-shard bytes. The expanded shard must use the same verifier/replay path and keep rejected controls out of exact rows.
outputs: Tests proving default shard byte stability and expanded generated topology counts
Replay-verified expanded JSONL artifact with deterministic row and digest accounting
acceptance: Default non-fixture shard SHA remains ae01fe8256a1c7a699e5f6f0358d499d6c1f4850148c9251e1c494bb059d3aa8
Expanded shard contains 11 curated exact rows, 30 generated exact rows, and 3 rejected controls unless a documented verifier gate rejects a candidate
Generated exact rows are balanced 10/10/10 across the depth-two topology families
Replay checks every exact composed-board row and skips only rejected controls

tasks.prompt: Generate and validate Partizan reports for the expanded non-fixture composition shard. Required reports: topology-family benchmark, generated-vs-curated source holdout, source baseline report, schema validation, and leakage validation. Reports must expose target-support and unseen generated labels.
outputs: Experiment matrix and verification gate updates
acceptance: All expanded reports have zero leakage violations under symmetry mode
Generated-vs-curated source holdout puts generated exact rows in test and curated exact rows in train/dev
Reports expose topology counts, source counts, unseen target labels, and rejected-row exclusion
Experiment matrix records the expanded artifact SHA, Astralbase commit, commands, non-claims, and falsification conditions

tasks.prompt: Strengthen deterministic baselines on the expanded composition shard. Baselines must include train-majority exact result, FEN/material majority, fixture-only component-sum sanity, source/topology holdout slices, target-support coverage, and clear abstention/failure accounting.
outputs: Tests for rejected-row exclusion, target-support reporting, and baseline abstention semantics
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py engine/orchestrator.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Every deterministic baseline is evaluated on the same expanded source holdout
Rejected rows are counted as controls but excluded from exact-value metrics
All unseen labels and abstentions are reported rather than hidden
Claim ledger states that deterministic baselines are floors, not decomposition-aware model evidence

tasks.prompt: Implement the first decomposition-aware learned baseline and matched controls on the expanded composition source holdout. Compare against full-board scalar, no-decomposition structured, train-majority, FEN/material, and hand-feature controls. Do not activate a decomposition-benefit claim unless the decomposition-aware model improves under the same split and target support.
outputs: Matched-split comparison table with target-support and failure analysis
acceptance: Model and controls use identical train/dev/test assignment and target field
Metrics include train/dev/test plus generated-source and topology slices
Failure to beat controls is documented as a blocker, not hidden
Any positive result cites the exact expanded shard SHA, commit, seed/config, and report path

tasks.prompt: Audit Waves 22-25 for NeurIPS-level claim discipline. Mark which claims are active, blocked, or falsified. Identify leakage risks, target-support weaknesses, hand-feature shortcuts, scale limitations, and missing baselines. Convert the next blocker into an executable follow-up wave.
outputs: Claim ledger update with active/inactive/falsified status
Reviewer-risk audit for the expanded composition benchmark
Next executable wave plan for agency/aesthetics or broader composition scale
acceptance: Every research claim points to exact artifact paths and verification commands
No agency, beauty, or decomposition-benefit claim is active without a baseline-beating result
Known failures and missing evidence are documented as blockers
The next wave has concrete gates rather than vague research intent

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_22_expanded_composition_benchmark.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The network and Wave 22-26 expanded composition plan are internally consistent.
The expanded non-fixture composition shard is generated and replay-verified.

integration_gates.command: python3 engine/ml_model.py composition-topology-benchmark-report /tmp/astralbase-w22-expanded-composition.jsonl --split-key-mode symmetry --fail-on-leakage --output docs/non_fixture_composition_topology_wave_22_benchmark_report.json && python3 engine/ml_model.py composition-holdout-report /tmp/astralbase-w22-expanded-composition.jsonl --holdout-selector composition_spec_source --holdout-value profiled_depth2_component_pair_generator_v0 --split-key-mode symmetry --fail-on-leakage --output docs/non_fixture_composition_source_wave_22_holdout_report.json
integration_gates.success_signal: Expanded composition topology and source-holdout reports pass leakage gates.

integration_gates.command: python3 engine/test.py && python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py engine/orchestrator.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py && cd ../astralbase && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
integration_gates.success_signal: Partizan reporting and Astralbase generation pass tests and static checks.
